text:
I recently applied for and received a Capital One Quicksilver MasterCard with a 0 % promotion rate for the first 15 months. My goal was to get a card that had a 0 % balance transfer rate so I could transfer a balance from another card and pay off the balance over time. In the materials for this card, it was stated that a balance could be transferred with {$0.00} fee at " the balance transfer rate ''. I've had credit cards for over 30 years and had never heard of a " balance transfer rate ''. People transfer balances to a card with 0 % interest for a fixed amount of time to pay it off and save interest. Happens every day. So I assumed the rate for the balance transfer would be at the 0 % promotional rate, since this is BY FAR the most common occurrence. 

In this case, the " balance transfer rate '' is the same as the regular rate for the card, 21.49 % ( what would take effect when the 0 % promotion ran out ). When I called Capital One to transfer the balance, I found out about the " balance transfer rate '' definition, and what my options were. There are NO other options. None. Take it or leave it. I asked to talk to a supervisor and got the same answer. There was NOTHING they can do to get me ANY type of 0 % balance transfer rate, even if I agree to pay a standard 3-5 % balance transfer fee ( which I was perfectly fine with once I realized the confusion with the language ). Their answer did not change. 

Honestly, what benefit is it to the consumer to transfer a balance to a card at the regular interest rate? Perhaps it would make sense if you had a much higher rate on the previous card, but even then it would make more sense to transfer the amount to a card with the 0 % balance transfer promotional rate. The idea and product are nonsensical. The fact that they bury this critical detail in semantics and hide it in the fine print to blur the interpretation for the policy, only then to offer no remedies of any kind, obscures the view for the consumer to the point tha

text:
I would like to comment on an unethical and inequitable banking practice that is legal and ubiquitous in the industry that financially disadvantages the least advantaged. 

This is the kind of issue that would require legislation to solve. Allow me to describe this as clearly as I can. First some facts as I understand : XXXX, I have XXXX forms of personal credit debt. 
+ Purchases + Cash Advances + Balance Transfers Each of these categories of credit has different interest rates. 

Each category of credit shows up as a separate fee on my statement. 

All three types of debt are expressed as a single balance. 

I am charged " fees '' XXXX every month rather than a single fee for the total balance. One for each category. 

Borrowers can not pay off individual categories of debt. This is an inequity. And probably unethical. At a minimum, it is crippling financially to the entire population. Lenders separate these categories with different terms, yet borrowers can't. How is that fair? 

So, if I borrow {$40.00} in a Cash Advance it is added to my debt of {$10000.00} from Purchases. The amount owed is {$10000.00}. There is then an additional fee of {$20.00} on my statement for interest. Let 's say my usual monthly contribution is {$100.00}. But, this month I pay my usual {$100.00} + the {$40.00} I borrowed + the {$20.00} fee. That's {$160.00}. 

And yet, I still see that {$20.00} for a Cash Advance EVERY MONTH until the ENTIRE {$10000.00} is paid off. They keep charging me at that addition ( higher ) interest rate on money that was NOT borrowed as a cash advance. 

In summary, I could end up paying $ XXXX on a {$40.00} cash withdrawal. How is this honest? When is enough enough? 

So, ok I transfer {$1000.00} to my card. Now, every month I have XXXX fees of {$20.00} to equal {$60.00} in monthly interest fees... forever ... even if I return the {$1000.00} and the {$40.00} plus interest within a month. Again, why should the {$10000.00} on XXXX be charged interest XXXX ways

text: I decided to stay in this hotel based on the location and it was only a short walk to the Lakeview area. Unfortunately Chicago has many hotels Downtown and inside the Loop but only a few options up north in the Lakeview/Wrigley Field area. After arriving to the hotel and checking into my room it was pretty clear that I paid too much for what I got. The room was a bit dated and my view looked directly into other hotel guest rooms, I had no city or street view at all. The internet worked well, the bed was low to the ground and not very comfy. On day 2 of my stay I received a call from the front desk stating they had a noise complaint coming from my room. I told him the only noise coming from my room was the phone when it rang being that I was sleep and there was no TV or radio on in my room and I was alone. He then stated that he was sure it was my room, at this point I told him to feel free to come up and inspect and I would be more than happy to let him in my room. The whole incident was seriously annoying but the bad service doesn't end there. After leaving the hotel and getting to the airport I realized I could not find my work cell phone. I called the desk and they transferred me to the housekeeping department. The person stated that they would have to wait until the housekeeper cleaning that room was done for the day before they would know if the phone was found. She stated she would call me back by the end of the day but I had heard nothing by the next day so I called back and left a message only to be ignored again. Of course it is my fault for leaving the phone but it is about the hotel service and how it was handled that disappointed me. I would not stay at this hotel again and due to elevated prices and limited options in the specific neighborhood I will get a 3 or 4 star room off Hotwire or Priceline for half the price and stay inside the loop.

text:
this is definitely one of the worst hotels i stayed in. it was a bunch of guys and we had a few rooms there. we were there for 3 nights. the first night there were ants in my room. i think someone had left a soda (in a plastic cup) out. id like to point out that no one spilled the soda, it was just in its plastic cub. regardless, you should not have ants after only being there a few hours. the first day was the only day we saw ants.

breakfast is in this weird tent that is actually not part of the hotel. it was cool in the mornings so we had to bundle up to even get a cup of coffee.

there was a gym and a parking lot underneath, but both were super sketchy. i wouldn't even bother going into the gym as it was located in a separate, but adjacent part of the hotel. bums walking off the street could have easily gotten in there.

as far as location goes, i think it was good. i wasn't the one driving so i never had to figure out how to get in/out of the hotel. LA is not the easiest place to drive, esp without a gps.

you definitely get exactly what you pay for. this place was cheap in so many ways. i do not recommend this hotel for families, couples or anyone beside a bunch of cheap college dudes who dont mind the overall cheapness of the location.

text: I opened a Credit Card account with Capital One to repair my credit after bankruptcy. I set up payments with my new XXXX XXXX XXXX checking account. Summary. This is a formal complaint against CapitalOne credit card for a UDAAP violation of Dodd-Frank due to their abusive practice in refusing to return a credit card overpayment of {$2300.00}. I have been seeking this reimbursement for more than two months, with no success.

Details. 
I am trying to receive {$2300.00} that I overpaid to Capital One credit card in making my XX/XX/XXXX credit card statement. CapitalOne refuses to return that overpayment : In a letter dated XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  they state : We received your request to refund your payment for {$2300.00} from XX/XX/XXXX. Unfortunately, your payment cant be refunded at this time. 

As background, that payment is the full amount due [ by XX/XX/XXXX ] shown on the XX/XX/XXXX credit card bill for my CapitalOne card ending in XXXX. I sent an online payment of {$2300.00} from my XXXX checking account on XX/XX/XXXX. I forgot that Id scheduled that autopayment, and so out of concern that I would be late on the monthly payment, I sent another full payment of {$2300.00} on XX/XX/XXXX from the same XXXX XXXXt. I subsequently realized I had double paid the credit card bill when I checked my XXXX checking account statement the next month. 

CapitalOne has refused to return the second payment to me, despite my repeated efforts, in lengthy half hour calls to their call center : I called in XXXX to make the point that I had double-paid my bill, and I wanted the second payment returned ; I was in the process of filing that claim with CapitalOne personnel, when the call was disconnected. 

I waited for my XXXXXXXX XXXX  statement to see if the claim had been processed and my account credited, but there was no such transaction. So later in XXXX I called again another half hour phone call -- where personnel assured me that my claim would be processed and a check would be mailed in the first week of XXXX. She made us feel very welcome and went to great lengths to explain she wanted our room to be perfect so needed a little time to ensure she could achieve this, when we returned we were given room 209.
During our coffee my wife and I were a little excited about the room we would be given given that our last room was excellent.

New York hotel rooms are generally small but this one was nowhere near the standard we had last year - it turns out that we were upgraded last year without being told (reception later confirmed this)which explained why we were so impressed on our previous visit, this time we had a standard room and whilst I know hotels in December aren't cheap if I'm paying almost $2000 for a 5 night stay I expect better than what we got.
At a guess I would say the room measures 150 square feet, and that includes bathroom area - this room also has no window, actually there is a small frosted window that you can't shift and it lets in virtually no natural light.
The room is also next door to a housekeeping cupboard and whilst we heard the housekeeping team I have to say we were never disturbed during sleeping hours.

text:
Service at the Fairmont Chicago is not their strong point and that’s disappointing for a Fairmont. The goal of this 2-night visit was to relax, read, and do nothing; with a lot of the time spent at the hotel. Big mistake.

This was my second stay at this property in less than a year. I thought the first stay was an aberration, but after this second stay, I’m mystified as to why it’s a popular place. I also stayed here a long time ago (in the early 2000’s), and it had the same type of service (or lack thereof) problems.

Arrival in the car: I wait, wait, and wait for someone (anyone!) to notice me and my rental car. And there were only two cars ahead of me and none behind me.

Check-in: I arrived at the 15th floor Gold Lounge check-in and I wait. And I wait, and wait, and wait. There are 4 other guests in this small lounge, but the attendant is nowhere to be found. She finally shows up, apologizes, etc.

Room: The first room on the 17th floor didn’t work. Again, one those small, cramped rooms that’s an afterthought of the builder or the architect. I noticed that this boxy thing they no longer call a “refrigerator,” but now called a “mini-bar” wasn’t cooling. They said someone from maintenance was coming and that person never appeared. I moved to another room. The second room, on the 15th floor, was much more comfortable, slightly larger, and the layout and spacing were much better suited to my planned relaxing weekend. I left a pair of pants in the 17th floor room, immediately returned to that room, and housekeeping had already touched up the room and taken the pants. I called the Gold Lounge and was told "they" (housekeeping) were on their way to my room with my pants. I wait, wait, and wait. Almost an hour later, I call the Gold Lounge, again, and I'm routed to the front desk. I can't remember the manager's name, but he tracked down the pants, but this was, yet, another hour later.

The Gold Lounge: I’m a tea drinker and they a thermos to hold hot water. A thermos.
